Home / Services / Casement Windows

Casement Windows

Maximize ventilation and enjoy unobstructed views with elegant casement windows.

Casement windows, hinged on one side and cranked outward, offer excellent ventilation and a clean, modern look for Long Beach homes. Their tight seal provides superior energy efficiency, crucial for our warm climate. We offer a variety of styles and finishes to complement any architectural design, from the sleek lines of contemporary condos near the waterfront to the classic charm of Craftsman homes in Bixby Knolls. Our installation process ensures a perfect fit, preventing drafts and leaks. We use durable, weather-resistant materials that can withstand the coastal environment. Casement windows are a great choice for areas where you want to maximize airflow and enjoy unobstructed views of the Pacific Ocean or the city skyline. We can also customize your casement windows with features like insect screens and decorative grids to enhance their functionality and aesthetic appeal. Our team will work closely with you to select the right casement windows for your needs and ensure a seamless installation process.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Consultation to determine the best casement window style and size
  2. 02 Step 2: Precise measurements to ensure a perfect fit
  3. 03 Step 3: Professional installation by certified technicians
  4. 04 Step 4: Quality inspection and cleanup

Benefits

  • Excellent ventilation and airflow
  • Unobstructed views
  • Enhanced energy efficiency
  • Modern and elegant design

Frequently Asked Questions

Are casement windows more energy-efficient than other types of windows?

Yes, casement windows generally offer superior energy efficiency due to their tight seal when closed.

Can casement windows be installed in any room?

Yes, casement windows can be installed in any room, but they are particularly well-suited for kitchens, bathrooms, and bedrooms.

What is the difference between a casement window and an awning window?

Casement windows are hinged on the side and crank outward, while awning windows are hinged at the top and swing outward.
